values.title: Global Relief Partners
values.hero_subtitle: Providing Lifesaving Aid to Communities in Crisis
values.hero_description: Global Relief Partners delivers rapid humanitarian assistance to communities affected by natural disasters and emergencies worldwide. From immediate response to long-term recovery, we stand with those who need us most.
values.cta_description: Your support enables us to respond within hours of a disaster, providing shelter, clean water, food, and medical supplies to families who have lost everything.

values.title: Hurricane Maria - Gulf Coast Response
values.body:
Global Relief Partners deployed emergency response teams within 12 hours of Hurricane Maria making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 4 storm, bringing 145 mph winds and devastating storm surge.

Response Activities

  • Established 15 emergency shelters housing 8,400 displaced residents
  • Distributed 120,000 meals and 50,000 gallons of clean water in the first week
  • Deployed mobile medical units providing care to 3,200 patients
  • Distributed 5,000 emergency supply kits (tarps, hygiene items, flashlights)
  • Coordinated search and rescue operations with local emergency services

Ongoing Recovery

As the emergency phase transitions to recovery, our teams are focusing on temporary housing solutions, debris removal support, and connecting affected families with federal disaster assistance programs.

values.title: Earthquake Response - Central Turkey
values.body:
A 7.1 magnitude earthquake struck central Turkey, causing widespread destruction in several provinces. Global Relief Partners activated its international rapid response team within hours.

Response Activities

  • Deployed search and rescue teams with specialized equipment
  • Established field hospitals treating 1,800 patients
  • Distributed 10,000 winterized shelter kits to families
  • Provided psychosocial support to 5,000 affected individuals
  • Set up water purification systems serving 25,000 people daily

Partnerships

We are coordinating closely with Turkish emergency management authorities (AFAD), the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA), and local NGO partners.

values.title: Midwest Flooding Response - Spring 2026
values.body:
Record spring rainfall and rapid snowmelt caused catastrophic flooding across the upper Midwest, affecting communities along the Mississippi and Missouri river systems.

Response Activities

  • Distributed 25,000 sandbags and flood barriers to at-risk communities
  • Evacuated 1,200 residents from flood zones using boats and vehicles
  • Opened 8 emergency shelters with capacity for 3,000 people
  • Provided hot meals and clean water to 15,000 displaced residents
  • Deployed damage assessment teams to 12 counties

Community Support

Our case managers are working directly with flood-affected families to help them navigate the recovery process, access FEMA assistance, and develop plans for rebuilding.

values.title: Family Emergency Preparedness Plan
values.body:
Every family should have an emergency plan in place before disaster strikes. This comprehensive guide walks you through creating a plan that will keep your family safe during any emergency.

What Your Plan Should Include

  • Communication plan: How your family will contact each other if separated
  • Evacuation routes: Primary and secondary routes from your home and workplace
  • Meeting points: Designated locations where family members will reunite
  • Emergency contacts: List of local emergency services, doctors, and out-of-area contacts
  • Pet plan: Arrangements for pets during evacuations

Emergency Kit Checklist

  • 72-hour supply of water (1 gallon per person per day)
  • Non-perishable food and manual can opener
  • First aid kit and prescription medications
  • Flashlight, batteries, and hand-crank radio
  • Important documents in a waterproof container

values.title: Community Resilience Toolkit
values.body:
The Community Resilience Toolkit provides local leaders, neighborhood organizations, and community groups with the tools and frameworks needed to prepare for, respond to, and recover from disasters.

Toolkit Components

  • Community risk assessment template
  • Volunteer coordination guide
  • Emergency communication plan for neighborhoods
  • Resource inventory worksheet
  • Post-disaster recovery planning framework

Building a Resilient Community

Research shows that communities with strong social networks and pre-established plans recover faster from disasters. This toolkit helps you build those connections before they are needed.

values.title: Disaster Response Volunteer Training Manual
values.body:
This manual provides comprehensive training materials for volunteers who wish to serve on Global Relief Partners disaster response teams.

Training Modules

  • Module 1: Introduction to disaster response and humanitarian principles
  • Module 2: Shelter management and displaced population support
  • Module 3: Emergency first aid and psychological first aid
  • Module 4: Mass feeding and water distribution operations
  • Module 5: Damage assessment and reporting
  • Module 6: Safety and security in disaster zones

Certification

Volunteers who complete all training modules and pass the assessment exam receive Disaster Response Volunteer certification, valid for three years.

image.alt: Volunteer training and disaster response preparation

values.title: Hurricane Maria Response - Week 3 Situation Report
values.body:
Three weeks after Hurricane Maria's devastating landfall, Global Relief Partners continues to provide critical support to affected communities across the Gulf Coast.

Key Updates

  • 15 shelters remain operational, housing 3,200 residents (down from 8,400 at peak)
  • Over 350,000 meals served to date
  • Mobile medical units have treated 5,800 patients since the response began
  • Debris removal operations underway in 8 counties
  • 1,200 families have received temporary housing assistance

Challenges

  • Power outages persist in 6 counties, affecting water supply and communications
  • Road damage limiting access to rural communities
  • Rising demand for mental health support services

Needs

We continue to need volunteers with construction and case management experience. Financial donations remain critical to sustaining long-term recovery operations.

image.alt: Hurricane recovery efforts in the Gulf Coast

values.title: Turkey Earthquake - Day 10 Situation Report
values.body:
Ten days after the devastating earthquake in central Turkey, search and rescue operations have concluded and the focus has shifted to providing sustained humanitarian assistance to affected populations.

Current Situation

  • Approximately 180,000 people displaced from their homes
  • Over 4,000 buildings severely damaged or destroyed
  • Temperatures dropping below freezing, increasing urgency for winter shelter

Our Response

  • 10,000 winterized shelter kits distributed to families
  • Field hospitals treating an average of 200 patients per day
  • 25 water purification systems operational, producing 500,000 liters daily
  • Psychosocial support teams providing counseling in 12 locations

Next Steps

We are scaling up transitional shelter construction and planning for medium-term recovery programming, including livelihood support and school rehabilitation.

values.title: Midwest Flooding - Initial Assessment Report
values.body:
Global Relief Partners has completed initial damage assessments in 12 counties across Iowa, Illinois, and Missouri following catastrophic spring flooding.

Assessment Findings

  • Estimated 6,500 homes with significant flood damage
  • 28,000 people displaced or directly affected
  • Agricultural losses affecting 200,000 acres of farmland
  • 18 road bridges damaged or impassable

Immediate Actions

  • Opened 8 emergency shelters in coordination with local emergency management
  • Deploying mobile kitchens to serve 5,000 meals daily
  • Distributing flood cleanup kits with mold prevention supplies
  • Activating volunteer teams for mud-out and debris removal operations

values.title: Carla Richardson
values.body:
Carla Richardson has served as Executive Director of Global Relief Partners since 2019, leading the organization through some of the most complex humanitarian crises of the past decade.

Background

Before joining GRP, Ms. Richardson spent 15 years with the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA), serving as Deputy Humanitarian Coordinator in three countries.

Vision

Under her leadership, GRP has expanded its disaster preparedness programs, invested in local capacity building, and pioneered the use of data analytics for more effective disaster response.

values.title: Ahmed Khalil
values.body:
Ahmed Khalil oversees all field operations for Global Relief Partners, coordinating response teams across multiple concurrent disasters and managing logistics for the organization's global supply chain.

Experience

Mr. Khalil has 20 years of experience in disaster response, including deployments to over 30 major disasters. He previously served as Operations Director for Doctors Without Borders in the Middle East region.

Expertise

  • Large-scale emergency logistics and supply chain management
  • Multi-agency coordination in complex emergencies
  • Field team deployment and security management

photo.alt: Director of Field Operations Ahmed Khalil

values.title: Jennifer Park
values.body:
Jennifer Park manages Global Relief Partners' volunteer program, recruiting, training, and deploying thousands of volunteers each year to disaster-affected communities.

Program Highlights

  • Grew volunteer corps from 8,000 to 18,000 active volunteers
  • Developed a comprehensive volunteer training curriculum
  • Created the Volunteer Rapid Response Network for 24-hour deployment capability
  • Established partnerships with 150 corporate volunteer programs

photo.alt: Director of Volunteer Services Jennifer Park

values.title: About Global Relief Partners
values.body:
Global Relief Partners (GRP) is a nonprofit humanitarian organization dedicated to helping communities prepare for, respond to, and recover from natural disasters and emergencies worldwide.

Our Mission

To deliver rapid, effective humanitarian assistance to disaster-affected communities while building long-term resilience and preparedness.

Our History

Founded in 2003, Global Relief Partners has grown from a small volunteer organization into a leading disaster response agency, responding to over 500 disasters in 32 countries and assisting more than 2.3 million people.

Our Approach

  • Rapid deployment within 24 hours of a disaster
  • Community-centered response that empowers local leadership
  • Long-term recovery support beyond the emergency phase
  • Investment in disaster preparedness and community resilience
  • Transparent and accountable use of donor funds

values.title: Support Our Work
values.body:
Your generous contribution enables Global Relief Partners to respond immediately when disaster strikes, providing shelter, food, clean water, and medical care to families who have lost everything.

How Your Donation Helps

  • $25 provides emergency supplies for one family for one week
  • $50 provides clean water for 100 people for one day
  • $100 provides a shelter kit for a displaced family
  • $250 funds a mobile medical team for one day
  • $500 funds emergency food distribution for 200 people

Ways to Give

  • One-time or recurring online donations
  • Corporate matching gift programs
  • Planned giving and bequests
  • In-kind donations of supplies and equipment

Global Relief Partners is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. All donations are tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law.
